Categories: Laboratory Animal Handling, Scales
Tag: Scales
Accuris™ Mini Balances are extraordinarily compact, easy to use, and convenient for a variety of laboratory weighing applications. With a footprint of only 2.6 x 3.9 inches, these Mini Balances offer exceptional accuracy and linearity through their full weighing ranges.
Available with either a 100g or 500g capacity, these digital balances feature an easy to read, backlit, liquid crystal display. Buttons for ON/OFF, MODE and TARE are located on the front for easy access. The software includes a calibration function for easy in-lab calibration with a 100g or 500g calibration weight. Calibration weights sold separately.
Designed for portability, the Accuris Mini Balances are powered by 2 x AAA batteries (included). After one minute sitting idle, the auto shut off feature turns off power to conserve battery life. Ideal for lab or field use, each balance includes a snap on protective cover with instructions conveniently printed on the underside.

Acrylic Brain Matrices
.single_variation_wrap{display:none !important;}Login to see prices
Item # RBMA-200C
Categories: Brain Matricies, Tissue Collection
Tag: Brain Matrices
Brain matrices were developed to provide medical researchers with the ability to precisely prepare discrete regions in fresh or preserved tissue samples.
Acrylic brain matrices are offered as durable and economical devices to prepare your tissue samples. Acrylic matrices are not recommended for heat or cold transfer and will become brittle if cooled or frozen.
We have selected our matrices to section tissue slices to a thickness as small as approximately 1 mm, depending upon what matrix design you choose to use. Ordinary industrial razor blades are generally used to slice the brain and/or tissue material. We recommend that the cutting blade be wetted with saline to act as a lubricant before shearing the target tissue. Micron tissue punches are also available for removal of specific areas of the tissues.
The coronal RBMs have the additional feature of a mid-line sagittal cut to facilitate the splitting of the left and right hemispheres. Sections can be as fine as 1 mm.
Repeatable slices to 1 mm providing precise blocking prior to sectioning

The Aeroneb® Lab Nebulizer uses a high-efficiency aerosolization technology for use in pre-clinical aerosol research and inhalation studies, providing a valuable link between pre-clinical and clinical product development.
Using Aerogen’s OnQ™ Aerosol Generator, the Aeroneb is designed to produce a highly respirable aerosol of virtually any drug in liquid. It produces a fine droplet, low-velocity aerosol without compressed air, and integrates easily into existing exposure and aerosol testing equipment.
Aeroneb is a registered trademark of Nektar Therapeutics.

A surgical plane of anesthetic depth is often defined by the loss of pedal reflex and confirmed by applying a stimulus to the paw pad. This anesthetic depth indicator is a sensory evaluator that standardizes common “toe-pinch” methods and is a good indicator of adequate anesthetic depth prior to surgery or other procedures.

Each endotracheal tube includes a safety wedge, designed to assist in insertion of the catheter into the trachea and minimize the chances of inserting the cannula too deeply. During intubation, the wedge will guide the catheter tip to the proper location between the larynx and carina. Once placed, the tight fit ensures bilateral ventilation and reduces air leaks from around the catheter.

Categories: Animal Holders, Laboratory Animal Handling
Tag: Mouse & Rat Restraints
Our low-cost, high-quality economy line of rodent holders is very useful for procedures requiring access to the tail, such as when performing simple injections and blood sampling.
To use, remove the tailgate and position the restrainer so the open end makes a 45° angle with the tabletop. Secure the rodent by the base of the tail and gently guide into the restrainer, forelimbs first. Apply gentle pressure to prevent the rodent from backing out of the restrainer. Once positioned, replace the tailgate into the groove to hold the animal in place.
If you are looking for CODA® Animal Holders, ideal for non-invasive blood pressure measurement and enhanced access to the tail, click here.

Categories: Warming, Water Recirculators
Tag: Water Recirculators
The HTP-1500 Heat Therapy Pump allows you to safely warm your animals. This is one of the most accurate localized heat therapy system available. Its bright and easy-to-read display provides continuous real-time temperature readings. The digital controller uses proprietary software to manage your prescribed set-point temperature to within 1.0°F. Three temperature safety limits guard against the possibility of overheating and the hi-limit switches can be tested without taking the unit apart! 8′ hose and couplings included.
This product is available in 110V configuration and only in the continental United States.
Pump connections are modified for compatibility with our TPZ-814EA warming pads.
Interested in better warming control using infrared heat? Click here.

Mouse and rat ear tags are used in research and lab animals, such as mice, rats, and other small animals. The ear tags are silver in color and made of a monel, nickel-copper alloy with excellent corrosion resistance, strength and ductility.
Laser etched ear tag characters provide greater visibility.
Custom orders
A minimum order of 1,000 ear tags is required for special numbering. Ear tag sequence always begins at 1, examples include 3001 to 4000, 4001 to 5000. If you require ear tag numbering that begins at “0” please specify on your order. Note that it may take additional time for processing.
Custom numbering and alpha characters incur additional fees. Please contact Customer Service at 888-572-8887 for a quotation.
Allow 10 to 14 weeks for delivery.

Item # TAGLD1-500
Categories: Ear Tags, Rodent Identification
Tag: Ear Tags
Mouse and rat ear tags are used in research and lab animals, such as mice, rats, and other small animals. The ear tags are silver in color and made of a monel, nickel-copper alloy with excellent corrosion resistance, strength and ductility.
Laser etched ear tag characters provide greater visibility. Condensed number typeface allows for up to four numbers on each side of the ear tag.
Custom orders
A minimum order of 1,000 ear tags is required for special numbering. Ear tag sequence always begins at 1, examples include 3001 to 4000, 4001 to 5000. If you require ear tag numbering that begins at “0” please specify on your order. Note that it may take additional time for processing.
Custom numbering and alpha characters incur additional fees. Please contact Customer Service at 888-572-8887 for a quotation.
Allow 10 to 14 weeks for delivery.

Stainless Steel Brain Matrices
.single_variation_wrap{display:none !important;}Login to see prices
Item # RBMS-200C
Categories: Brain Matricies, Tissue Collection
Tag: Brain Matrices
Brain matrices were developed to better help the medical research community prepare discrete regions in fresh or preserved tissue samples.
The more durable stainless steel matrices are useful when heat transfer or the maintenance of a specific temperature range is required in your scientific protocol.
We have selected our matrices to section tissue slices to a thickness as small as approximately 0.5 mm (sections can vary from 0.5 to 0.75 mm), depending upon what brain matrix design you choose to use. Ordinary industrial razor blades are generally used to slice the brain and/or tissue material. We recommend that the cutting blade be wetted with saline to act as a lubricant before shearing the target tissue. Micron tissue punches are also available for removal of specific areas of the tissues.
The coronal RBMs have the additional feature of a mid-line sagittal cut to facilitate the splitting of the left and right hemispheres. Sections can be as fine as 1 mm.
Repeatable slices to 0.5 mm providing precise blocking prior to sectioning
Can be chilled or sterilized

Mouse and rat ear tags are used in research and lab animals, such as mice, rats, and other small animals. The ear tags are silver in color and made of a monel, nickel-copper alloy with excellent corrosion resistance, strength and ductility.
Note: Numbering longer than 3 characters will wrap around to the other side of the tag.
Custom orders

Custom numbering and alpha characters incur additional fees. Please contact Customer Service at 888-572-8887 for a quotation.
Allow 10 to 14 weeks for delivery.

Categories: Animal Holders, Laboratory Animal Handling
Tag: Mouse & Rat Restraints
Our low-cost, high-quality economy line of rodent holders is very useful for procedures requiring access to the tail, such as when performing simple injections and blood sampling.
To use, remove the nosepiece and position the restrainer so that the slit is facing up. Secure the rodent by the base of the tail and gently guide into the restrainer, hind limbs first. Once positioned, replace the nosepiece into the groove and tighten with the screw.
If you are looking for CODA® Animal Holders, ideal for non-invasive blood pressure measurement and enhanced access to the tail, click here.

Kent Scientific’s small animal and rodent anesthesia machine is one of the easiest to use and most economical gas anesthesia systems on the market.
Perfect for laboratory and veterinary anesthesia delivery, the VetFlo™ anesthesia system is completely self-contained and portable. The multi-channel base unit is designed for multiple stations that are individually controlled, operating off of one vaporizer. With an easy-to-follow chart, each station can deliver a different flow rate of anesthesia.
Dimensions: 13″ H x 14.625″ W x 10″ D

Item # PS-PWTY-1
Category: Warranty
For labs that can’t afford downtime, the Premium Warranty offers the ultimate protection—two additional years of full coverage plus unlimited use of loaner equipment with next-day shipping. You’ll also receive onsite installation and training for your team and enjoy 24-hour expedited repairs for the fastest possible turnaround.
Note:
Damage or failures that result from abuse, negligence, or misuse are not covered under warranty.
Expedited repair time starts the business day after product is received and ends when shipped to customer.
Customer is responsible to inform Kent Scientific when making a warranty claim.
Extended warranties purchased after the original system sale are subject to approval. It may be necessary to have the controller returned for inspection before the purchase of the extended warranty.
